design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate rail-shooter stages while managing shield/damage, destroy enemies, assist wingmen, defeat boss, progress to next level via chosen route
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing offensive focus (destroying enemies for score) against defensive necessity (protecting shield, protecting wingmen) while maintaining forward momentum on rails
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite mercenary pilot commanding an elite fighter squad against overwhelming odds, making tactical decisions about engagement priorities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering 3D rail shooter that rejects free-form flight for structured narrative paths; player choice is constrained to three predetermined difficulty-based routes rather than open exploration; wingman mechanics embed squad leadership into moment-to-moment gameplay; damage model (incremental shield loss, wing breakage affecting handling) creates mechanical consequence for poor piloting

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives displayed in HUD; wingmen can be permanently lost if shot down during stage, reducing available support for remainder of playthrough
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-stage checkpointing; boss encounters reset if player loses all lives
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: player selects one of three routes at game start, each with distinct difficulty curve and unique level series; within stages, player chooses whether to assist wingmen (easier playthrough with squad support vs harder solo focus)

progression.upgrade_system: Wingman assistance unlocks additional enemy engagement opportunities; power-ups scattered throughout stages (ammunition, shield restoration, temporary buffs); weapon upgrades available if ship wings remain intact
progression.unlock_mechanism: Three branching routes through Lylat system; all paths share Corneria (first) and Venom (final) but diverge with unique intermediate levels based on difficulty selection
replayability_hooks: Three distinct routes with unique level variants encourage multiple playthroughs
Score-based ranking system rewards enemy destruction and wingman survival
Locational damage mechanic (wing clipping) creates skill expression opportunities for optimization
Wingman survival state persists throughout playthrough, creating emergent difficulty scaling

world_building_techniques: Wingman radio chatter during missions establishes squad camaraderie and personality
Boss encounters named after planets in Lylat system reinforce world geography
Anthropomorphic animal cast (foxes, frogs, birds, rabbits, apes) establish cohesive fictional society
Pre-scripted wingman encounter patterns create sense of coordinated military operation

boss_design.philosophy: Thematic culmination of each level's environmental and enemy design; boss encounters isolate player from wingman support, forcing solo engagement; bosses represent escalating threat levels across the three difficulty routes
notable_bosses: Route-specific environmental bosses tied to each path's unique levels

faction_indicators: Star Fox team (player-aligned mercenary unit: Fox, Falco, Peppy, Slippy)
Corneria defense force (command structure under General Pepper)
Andross forces (antagonist army across Lylat system)
danger_signals: Wingman distress calls indicate immediate threat to squad members
Escalating enemy variety and density across routes signals advancing conflict intensity
Boss encounters represent peak threat moments requiring full pilot capability
environmental_storytelling: Three routes through Lylat system represent different strategic approaches to Andross threat
Venom planet as final destination represents heart of enemy territory
Corneria as shared starting point establishes home world defense context
Stage environment design reflects planet characteristics and Andross occupation
